en: Therefore, since we are surrounded by so great a cloud of witnesses, let us also lay aside every weight, and sin which clings so closely, and let us run with endurance the race that is set before us,
zh: 所以，我們既然有這麼多的見證人，像雲彩圍繞著我們，就應該脫下各樣的拖累，和容易纏住我們的罪，以堅忍的心奔跑那擺在我們面前的賽程；

en: looking to Jesus, the founder and perfecter of our faith, who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising the shame, and is seated at the right hand of the throne of God.
zh: 專一注視耶穌，就是那位信心的創造者和完成者。他因為那擺在面前的喜樂，就忍受了十字架，輕看了羞辱，現在就坐在 神寶座的右邊。

en: Consider him who endured from sinners such hostility against himself, so that you may not grow weary or fainthearted.
zh: 這位忍受罪人那樣頂撞的耶穌，你們要仔細思想，免得疲倦灰心。

en: In your struggle against sin you have not yet resisted to the point of shedding your blood.
zh: 你們與罪惡鬥爭，還沒有對抗到流血的地步；

en: And have you forgotten the exhortation that addresses you as sons? "My son, do not regard lightly the discipline of the Lord, nor be weary when reproved by him.
zh: 你們又忘記了那勸你們好像勸兒子的話，說： “我兒！你不可輕看主的管教， 受責備的時候也不要灰心；

en: For the Lord disciplines the one he loves, and chastises every son whom he receives."
zh: 因為主所愛的，他必管教， 他又鞭打所收納的每一個兒子。”

en: It is for discipline that you have to endure. God is treating you as sons. For what son is there whom his father does not discipline?
zh: 為了接受管教，你們要忍受，因為 神待你們好像待兒子一樣；哪有兒子不受父親管教的呢？

en: If you are left without discipline, in which all have participated, then you are illegitimate children and not sons.
zh: 作兒子的都受過管教。如果你們沒有受管教，就是私生子，不是兒子了。

en: Besides this, we have had earthly fathers who disciplined us and we respected them. Shall we not much more be subject to the Father of spirits and live?
zh: 還有，肉身的父親管教我們，我們尚且敬重他們；何況那萬靈的父，我們不是更要順服他而得生嗎？

en: For they disciplined us for a short time as it seemed best to them, but he disciplines us for our good, that we may share his holiness.
zh: 肉身的父親照著自己的意思管教我們，只有短暫的日子；唯有 神管教我們，是為著我們的好處，使我們在他的聖潔上有分。

en: For the moment all discipline seems painful rather than pleasant, but later it yields the peaceful fruit of righteousness to those who have been trained by it.
zh: 但是一切管教，在當時似乎不覺得快樂，反覺得痛苦；後來卻為那些經過這種操練的人，結出平安的果子來，就是義。

en: Therefore lift your drooping hands and strengthen your weak knees,
zh: 所以，你們要把下垂的手和發軟的腿挺直起來；

en: and make straight paths for your feet, so that what is lame may not be put out of joint but rather be healed.
zh: 也要把你們所走的道路修直，使瘸子不至於扭腳，反而得到復原。

en: Strive for peace with everyone, and for the holiness without which no one will see the Lord.
zh: 你們要竭力尋求與眾人和睦，並且要竭力追求聖潔。如果沒有聖潔，誰也不能見主。

en: See to it that no one fails to obtain the grace of God; that no "root of bitterness" springs up and causes trouble, and by it many become defiled;
zh: 你們要小心，免得有人失去了 神的恩典；免得有苦根長起來纏繞你們，因而污染了許多人；

en: that no one is sexually immoral or unholy like Esau, who sold his birthright for a single meal.
zh: 又免得有人成為淫亂的和貪戀世俗的，好像以掃一樣，為了一點點食物，竟把自己長子的名分出賣了。

en: For you know that afterward, when he desired to inherit the blessing, he was rejected, for he found no chance to repent, though he sought it with tears.
zh: 你們知道，後來以掃想要承受祝福，卻被拒絕了；他雖然帶著眼淚尋求，還是沒有反悔的餘地。

en: For you have not come to what may be touched, a blazing fire and darkness and gloom and a tempest
zh: 你們不是來到那座摸得著的山。那裡有烈火、密雲、幽暗、暴風、

en: and the sound of a trumpet and a voice whose words made the hearers beg that no further messages be spoken to them.
zh: 號筒的響聲和說話的聲音；那些聽見這聲音的人，都請求 神不要再向他們多說話；

en: For they could not endure the order that was given, "If even a beast touches the mountain, it shall be stoned."
zh: 因為他們擔當不起那命令：“就是走獸挨近這山，也要用石頭把牠打死。”

en: Indeed, so terrifying was the sight that Moses said, "I tremble with fear."
zh: 當時，顯出的景象是那麼可怕，連摩西也說：“我非常恐懼戰兢。”

en: But you have come to Mount Zion and to the city of the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem, and to innumerable angels in festal gathering,
zh: 你們卻是來到錫安山和永活的 神的城，就是天上的耶路撒冷；在那裡有千萬的天使聚集，

en: and to the assembly of the firstborn who are enrolled in heaven, and to God, the judge of all, and to the spirits of the righteous made perfect,
zh: 有名字登記在天上眾長子的教會，有審判眾人的 神，有被成全的義人的靈魂，

en: and to Jesus, the mediator of a new covenant, and to the sprinkled blood that speaks a better word than the blood of Abel.
zh: 有新約的中保耶穌，還有他所灑的血。這血所傳的信息比亞伯的血所傳的更美。

en: See that you do not refuse him who is speaking. For if they did not escape when they refused him who warned them on earth, much less will we escape if we reject him who warns from heaven.
zh: 你們要謹慎，不要棄絕那位說話的，因為從前的人棄絕了那位在地上警戒他們的，尚且不能逃罪；何況現在我們背棄那位從天上警戒我們的呢？

en: At that time his voice shook the earth, but now he has promised, "Yet once more I will shake not only the earth but also the heavens."
zh: 當時他的聲音震動了地；現在他卻應許說：“下一次，我不但要震動地，還要震動天。”

en: This phrase, "Yet once more," indicates the removal of things that are shaken- that is, things that have been made- in order that the things that cannot be shaken may remain.
zh: “下一次”這句話，是表明那些被震動的，要像被造之物那樣被除去，好使那些不能震動的可以留存，

en: Therefore let us be grateful for receiving a kingdom that cannot be shaken, and thus let us offer to God acceptable worship, with reverence and awe,
zh: 因此，我們既然領受了不能震動的國，就應該感恩，照著 神所喜悅的，用虔誠敬畏的心事奉他；

en: for our God is a consuming fire.
zh: 因為我們的 神是烈火。
